Interface OrderService

All Known Implementing Classes:
OrderServiceImpl
public interface OrderService
Service interface defining all business operations for the Order domain.

Covers the full order lifecycle: placement, pre-trade validation, routing, execution fills, allocations, and cancellation.

Version:
2.0
Author:
WealthPro Team

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    AllocationResponseDTO
    allocateOrder(Long orderId, AllocationRequestDTO requestDTO)
    Allocate a FILLED order to a client account.
    OrderResponseDTO
    cancelOrder(Long orderId)
    Cancel an order.
    OrderResponseDTO
    createOrder(OrderRequestDTO requestDTO)
    Create a new order with status PLACED.
    void
    deleteOrder(Long orderId)
    Delete an order by ID.
    ExecutionFillResponseDTO
    executeFill(Long orderId, ExecutionFillRequestDTO requestDTO)
    Record an execution fill and auto-update order status (PARTIALLY_FILLED or FILLED based on cumulative fill quantity).

    getAllOrders()
    Retrieve all orders.
    OrderResponseDTO

    Retrieve an order by its ID.
    OrderLifecycleDTO
    getOrderLifecycle(Long orderId)
    Retrieve the full lifecycle view of an order — the order itself plus all related pre-trade checks, execution fills, and allocations.

    getOrdersByClientId(Long clientId)
    Retrieve all orders for a specific client.

    Retrieve all orders with a given status.

    Route a VALIDATED order to a simulated exchange venue.

    Run all four pre-trade compliance checks (SUITABILITY, LIMIT, EXPOSURE, CASH) against the specified order.
    OrderResponseDTO
    updateOrder(Long orderId, OrderRequestDTO requestDTO)
    Update an existing order's details.

  • Method Details

    • createOrder

      OrderResponseDTO createOrder(OrderRequestDTO requestDTO)
      Create a new order with status PLACED.

    • getOrderById

      OrderResponseDTO getOrderById(Long orderId)
      Retrieve an order by its ID.

    • getAllOrders

      List<OrderResponseDTO> getAllOrders()
      Retrieve all orders.

    • getOrdersByClientId

      List<OrderResponseDTO> getOrdersByClientId(Long clientId)
      Retrieve all orders for a specific client.

    • getOrdersByStatus

      List<OrderResponseDTO> getOrdersByStatus(OrderStatus status)
      Retrieve all orders with a given status.

    • updateOrder

      OrderResponseDTO updateOrder(Long orderId, OrderRequestDTO requestDTO)
      Update an existing order's details.

    • deleteOrder

      void deleteOrder(Long orderId)
      Delete an order by ID.

    • runAllPreTradeChecks

      List<PreTradeCheckResponseDTO> runAllPreTradeChecks(Long orderId)
      Run all four pre-trade compliance checks (SUITABILITY, LIMIT, EXPOSURE, CASH) against the specified order. Auto-transitions the order to VALIDATED if all pass, or REJECTED if any fail.
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order to validate
      Returns:
      list of check results

    • routeOrder

      OrderResponseDTO routeOrder(Long orderId)
      Route a VALIDATED order to a simulated exchange venue. Transitions the order to ROUTED.
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order to route
      Returns:
      updated order with routedVenue and ROUTED status

    • executeFill

      ExecutionFillResponseDTO executeFill(Long orderId, ExecutionFillRequestDTO requestDTO)
      Record an execution fill and auto-update order status (PARTIALLY_FILLED or FILLED based on cumulative fill quantity).
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order being filled
      requestDTO - fill details
      Returns:
      the recorded fill

    • allocateOrder

      AllocationResponseDTO allocateOrder(Long orderId, AllocationRequestDTO requestDTO)
      Allocate a FILLED order to a client account.
      Parameters:
      orderId - the filled order
      requestDTO - allocation details
      Returns:
      the recorded allocation

    • cancelOrder

      OrderResponseDTO cancelOrder(Long orderId)
      Cancel an order. Only orders in PLACED, VALIDATED, or ROUTED status can be cancelled.
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order to cancel
      Returns:
      updated order with CANCELLED status

    • getOrderLifecycle

      OrderLifecycleDTO getOrderLifecycle(Long orderId)
      Retrieve the full lifecycle view of an order — the order itself plus all related pre-trade checks, execution fills, and allocations.
      Parameters:
      orderId - the order ID
      Returns:
      comprehensive lifecycle DTO
